steps: In both cases prior use: Clean Human Tissue handling hood with 0.2 Molarity (M) NaOH, 10% Presept, 70% EtOH and dH 2 O.

Transfer all materials needed and handle human brain samples carefully in ahuman handling hood. Critical Notes : - The Invent Biotech BN-020 kit was initially created for customers to avoid sorting.According to the manufacturer, the kit works with as little as 1 mg of neuronaltissue or cells. However, in our case, we adapted it to isolate nuclei faster and more consistently than the manual method, which requires hands-on preparation of the buffers to be used. Due to our technical needs for cell-type selection and the difficulty of weighing very small amounts of frozen tissue, we use a minimum of 10 mg (usually 10-30mg) of frozen post-mortem brain tissue.Furthermore, as our downstream aim is to do single-cell whole genome amplification of selected nuclei, the use of this kit is followed by nuclei immunostaining and sorting. Due to that, we use the BN-020 kit according to manufacturer instructions with minor modifications. - According to the Invent Biotech protocol, the Buffer B steps (here 6.9 - 6.13) are optional, but we use them to prepare the cleanest nuclei population possible. - All centrifugation steps in this part of the protocol can be performed at Room temperature ..
Prepare 5% BSA in PBS (1x) and store at 4 °C .
Prepare PIC (1x complete EDTA-free Protease Inhibitor Cocktail) and store in the freezer or remove an aliquot from the freezer and place it On ice .
Use the Invent Biotech BN-020 kit according to manufacturer instructions with minor modifications as follows:

Cut small brain pieces of tissue of interest in pre-weighed tubes and weigh the tissue on a scale aiming for 10 mg - 30 mg of each tissue/donor depending on the downstream needs. - The kit can be used for smaller starting material, but we used aminimum 10 mg inorder to be able to scale it properly and have enough nuclei for the downstreamapplication. - If a larger volume of starting material is needed to avoid potential liquid retention in the filter, we suggest splitting the brain pieces into different tubes for nuclei isolation. Then, pool the nuclei together in the same tube before immunostaining.
Add 200 µL of cold buffer A in each tube containing a tissue and place it On ice . From now on, place tubes On ice , if not stated differently.
Incubate the tube On ice for 00:05:00 and carefully transfer homogenate to a filter (column) in the collection tube (avoid larger debris that sink to the bottom of the tube).
Incubate the tube with the cap open at -20 °C for 00:07:00 . According to the manufacturer protocol, the incubation time can vary between 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 .
Cap the filter and immediately centrifuge at 13000 x g, , 30 seconds .

Discard the filter (column) and resuspend the pellet by pipetting up and down gently ,10-20 times. - Try to avoid lipids that attach to the wall of the tube. - If there is a liquid retention in the filter reduce the amount of starting material by half. Figure 1. Sample after centrifugation with the filter column.
Centrifuge at 600 x g, , 5 minutes . The pellet may not be obvious as these are isolated nuclei and it may be in the side close to the bottom of the tube.

Pour out the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 200 µL PBS with 5% BSA that will be overlaid on top of buffer B in the next step. Add 1 mL cold buffer B to a 1.5 ml eppendorf tube. Remove bubbles if present.
Carefully overlay the 200 µL nuclear suspension on top of buffer B by slowly expelling the nuclear suspension against the wall of the tube.

Centrifuge the tube at 1000 x g, , 10 minutes . After centrifugation, cellular debris, oil, and myelin will stay on the top (white-milky layer). The purified nuclei are found in the pellet. The nuclei pellet may not be visible. This depends on the brain region used.
Carefully remove the milky layer by withdrawing it into a 1 ml pipette tip and discard the rest of the supernatant.
Pour out the remaining buffer B, leaving 50 µL in the bottom of the ultracentrifuge tube (as it contains the nuclear fraction).
Resuspend the pellet in 50 µL - 200 µL PBS containing Blocking Buffer. Ensure to rinse the wall of the tube to collect all nuclei.

When discarding the supernatants after each centrifugation step, leave the last 50 µL at the bottom. Resuspend in same volumes as in prior step to keep the final volume consistent between the steps, but on the last centrifugation step of this section increase the volume to have at least 100 µL in each tube.
Incubate nuclei in blocking buffer for 00:30:00 at 4 °C in a rotating wheel or in falcon tubes in a tube roller.
Add 1ry antibodies or conjugated antibodies directly to the blocking buffer.

Incubate the samples for at least 00:30:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking. Alternatively, incubation can be done for 01:00:00 or over-night. Prepare negative control for the immunostaining, e.g., split the sample to 150 µL with 1ry antibodies and 50 µL without 1ry antibodies as negative control.
Pellet nuclei at 800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and carefully discard supernatant.
Resuspended in pre-chilled blocking buffer for a gentle wash.
Re-pellet nuclei at 800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and discard supernatant.

Add 2ry antibodies (e.g., AF488 1:500) and DAPI (0.1 µg/ml) in blocking buffer and incubate for at least 00:30:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking. If conjugated antibodies were used in step 10, skip this step. DAPI concentration may need to be optimized based on sample and sorter used.
Wash nuclei again with blocking buffer.

Pellet nuclei at 800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and carefully discard the supernatant. Resuspend nuclei in sorting buffer and proceed to filtering using Flowmi Cell Strainers in clean 1.5 mL eppendorf tubes. If the sorting is scheduled for another day, resuspend nuclei in Nuclei Storage Buffer and store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. The day of sorting, re-stain the nuclei with DAPI and filter sterilize.

Prepare 96-well collection that are compatible with your lab PCR machine by placing 5 µL of TE Buffer in each well and seal very carefully using VersiCap Mat strips. We use 96-well cuttable plates (Starlabs E1403-0100), but other plates such as low bind 96-well plates are recommended.
Transfer nuclei and collection plates on an ice-bucket to the sorting facility. In our case, we use BD FACSAria Fusion Cell Sorter instrument at the UCL Cancer Institute Flow Cytometry facility.

Acquire small amounts of nuclei from all samples and perform a batch analysis to assess the sample quality and identify the targeted populations for sorting. Use negative control samples as threshold references. If possible, keep a consistent gating position across the samples from different donors. Select gating parameters to isolate the singlets from the overall detected particles by selecting forward (FSC) and side scatter (SSC), FCS single cell gate and SSC single cell gate. Then select the nuclei by their DAPI expression. From the nuclei population (DAPI+), apply further gating parameters based on the antibodies used. Figure 3. Examples of the gating profiles when sorting human post-mortem nuclei from cingulate cortex stained with NeuN antibodies (A) isolated manually or (B) by using the (B) BN-020 kit. The FCS files were analysed on FlowJo. Figure 4. Examples human post-mortem nuclei (A) Cingulate cortex and (B) substantia nigra tissues stained with the conjugated antibodies (A) NeuN-AF488 or (B) Olig2-AF647 (left panel) or their isotype controls (right panel).
Centrifuge the collection plates for 00:00:10 at low speed to ensure reagent at bottom.

Sort the single-nuclei of interest into the centrifuged 96-well collection plates. Before single cell sorting, use Accudrop beads for a test sort to evaluate the position of the plate and to ensure the sorted cells will be deposited into each well accurately in the middle.
Cap the collection plates carefully to ensure all sorted cells are merged in the reagent and not attached to the side of tube.
Centrifuge the collection plates and place them On ice for transportation to the laboratory. You can directly proceed to Section 4 or store the plates at -70 °C .
Thaw collection plates On ice .
Centrifuge the collection plates briefly prior use.
Perform single-nucleus Whole Genome Amplification according to Takara’s guidelines. We observe approximately 85-100% amplification success rate depending on the sample/donor used.
Prepare compatible libraries for sequencing, such as Agilent SureSelect XT HS2, Illumina DNA PCR-Free prep or other DNA library preparation kit.
Perform library pooling and sequence the libraries.
Before starting: Clean Dounce tissue grinder (prior to use, between samples, and at the end of the experiment) with 0.2 Molarity (M) NaOH, 10% Presept, 70% EtOH and dH 2 O, and let them dry.
UV sterilize and clean PCR hood (to be used for buffer preparation) with 10% Presept, 70% EtOH and dH 2 O.
Pre-cool centrifuge at 4 °C .

Prepare the NIM (Nuclear Isolation Media), ODN (Optiprep Diluent for Nuclei) buffers, 25% and 29% iodixanol solutions in a PCR hood. Keep On ice until use: NIM (Nuclear Isolation Media): %s %s ODN (Optiprep Diluent for Nuclei): %s %s 25% iodixanol solution: NIM:ODGM:ODN 6:5:1. 29% iodixanol solution: ODGM: ODN 29:31.
Transfer the buffers and Dounce tissue grinder in the human handling hood.
Transfer 20 mg - 50 mg frozen brain tissue to a microcentrifuge tube containing 500 µL of NIM using a pair of forceps.

Optional step: Mincing the tissue in small fragments over a glass plate on ice using a scalpel could significantly decrease the time needed for homogenization and facilitate the trituration with the pipette tips.

Gently triturate the tissue with a cut pipette tip (1ml tip). Repeat several times with pipette tips with different-cut pipette tips (estimate 3-4 different cut sizes). The tissue is perfectly dissociated once it can pass through an uncut pipette tip.
Measure the total volume using 1 ml pipette.
Add Triton X-100 solution to the sample to achieve final concentration 0.1% (stock solution 10%).

Homogenize using 2 ml Dounce tissue grinders: Grinder A (loose, large clearance pestles): 2-3 strokes. Grinder B (tight, small clearance pAt this point, the sample solution should appear homogeneous, butestles): 8-12 strokes. The sample solution should appear homogeneous at this point, but the use of the Dounce grinder allows nuclei to remain intact.
Transfer the homogenate to a fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge at 1000 x g, 4 °C, 8 minutes . Discard supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet gently in 700 µL 25% Iodixanol Solution.

Layer the sample (avoiding mixing the phases) onto 700 µL of 29% iodixanol solution. Use 1 mL syringe without a needle to slowly load and suspend the sample on top. As a result, 2 clearly defined phases should appear as follows (Figure 2). Figure 2. Nuclear isolation using iodixanol gradient . A) Homogenization using the Dounce tissue grinders. B) Gradient generation solution is generated from 25% iodixanol gradient containing the cell lysate on the top and 29% iodixanol gradient solution on the bottom.
Spin at 10300 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes . Remove the supernatant, leaving 50 µL in the bottom of the ultracentrifuge tube (as it contains the nuclear fraction).

Pellet nuclei at 800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and carefully discard supernatant. Then, resuspended in pre-chilled Blocking Buffer for a gentle wash. Critical notes : When discarding the supernatants after each centrifugation step, leave the last 50 µL at the bottom. Resuspend in same volumes as in prior step to keep the final volume consistent between the steps, but on the last centrifugation step of this section increase the volume to at least 100 µL in each tube.
Incubate nuclei in Blocking Buffer for 00:30:00 at 4 °C in a rotating wheel or falcon tubes in a tube roller.
Add 1ry antibodies or conjugated antibodies directly to the Blocking Buffer.

Incubate the samples for at least 00:30:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking. - Alternatively, incubation can be done for 01:00:00 or over-night. - Prepare negative control for the immunostaining, e.g., split the sample to 150 µL with 1ry antibodies and 50 µL without 1ry antibodies as a negative control.
Re-pellet nuclei at 800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and discard supernatant.

Add 2ry antibodies (e.g., AF488 1:500) and DAPI (0.1 µg/ml) in blocking buffer and incubate for at least 00:30:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking. Critical notes : If conjugated antibodies were used in step 10, skip this step. DAPI concentration may need to be optimized based on the sample and the sorter used.
Rewash nuclei with Blocking Buffer.

Pellet nuclei at and carefully discard the supernatant. Resuspend nuclei in Sorting Buffer and filter the nuclei suspension using Flowmi Cell Strainers in clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. If the sorting is scheduled for another day, resuspend nuclei in Nuclei Storage Buffer and store the nuclei in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. On the day of sorting, re-stain the nuclei with DAPI and filter sterilize.

Prepare 96-well collection plates (compatible with your lab PCR machine) by placing 5 µL of TE Buffer in each well and seal them very carefully using VersiCap Mat strips or plate sealers,. We use 96-well cuttable plates (Starlabs E1403-0100), but other plates, such as low-bind 96-well plates, are recommended.
Transfer nuclei and collection plates on an ice-bucket to the sorting facility. We use the We use the BD FACSAria Fusion Cell Sorter instrument at the UCL Cancer Institute Flow Cytometry facility.

Acquire small amounts of nuclei from all samples and perform a batch analysis to assess the sample quality and identify the targeted populations for sorting. Key steps: Use negative control samples as threshold references. If possible, keep a consistent gating position across the samples from different donors. Select gating parameters to isolate the singlets from the overall detected particles by selecting forward (FSC) and side scatter (SSC), FCS single cell gate, and SSC single cell gate. Then, choose the nuclei by their DAPI expression. From the nuclei population (DAPI+), apply further gating parameters based on the antibodies used. Figure 3. Examples of the gating profiles when sorting human post-mortem nuclei from cingulate cortex stained with NeuN antibodies and DAPI. A) isolated manually, B) by using BN-020 kit. The FCS files were analysed with FlowJo. Figure 4. Examples of human post-mortem brain nuclei. A) Cingulate cortex samples stained with conjugated NeuN-AF488 antibodies (left panel) or isotype control antibodies (right panel). B) Substantia nigra samples stained with the conjugated Olig2-AF647 antibodies (left panel) or their isotype control antibodies (right panel). All nuclei were stained with DAPI.
Centrifuge the collection plates for 00:00:10 at low speed to ensure reagent at bottom.

Sort the single-nuclei of interest into the centrifuged 96-well collection plates. Before single-cell sorting, use Accudrop beads for a test sort to evaluate the plate's position and ensure the sorted cells will be deposited into each well accurately in the middle.
Cap the collection plates carefully to ensure all sorted cells are merged in the reagent and not attached to the side of the tube.
Centrifuge the collection plates and place them On ice for transportation to the laboratory. You can directly proceed to Section 4 or store the plates at -70 °C .
Thaw collection plates On ice .
Centrifuge the collection plates briefly before use.

Perform single-nucleus Whole Genome Amplification according to Takara’s guidelines and purify the amplicons in an AMPure XP beads/sample ratio 1:1. We have observed approximately 85-100% amplification success rate (based on amplification QC, such as Qubit and TapeStation measurements), depending on the sample/donor used.
Prepare compatible libraries for sequencing using Agilent SureSelect XT HS2, Illumina DNA PCR-Free, or another DNA library preparation kit.
Perform library pooling and sequence the libraries.
Homogenize the tissue using the pestle provided by grinding gently with twisting force 50-60 times. Then, add 500 µL cold buffer A to the tube and continue to grind 20-30 times.
Clean pestles before, between, and after use with 0.2 Molarity (M) NAOH, 10% Presept and dH 2 O and let them air-dry before use.
